content: pi-kit repo structure: extensions/, skills/, prompts/, themes/, bin/. Extensions are TypeScript (index.ts), skills are markdown (SKILL.md), prompts are .md files.
Extension pattern: default export function receiving ExtensionAPI, registers tools via pi.registerTool() and commands via pi.registerCommand(). See extensions/render/index.ts for reference.

Cleave CLI invoked via: cleave assess, cleave run, cleave architect, cleave probe, cleave check-permissions. Installed via pipx install styrene-cleave. State machine: PREFLIGHT→ASSESS→ROUTE→PLAN→REVIEW→EXECUTE→REPORT. See skills/cleave/SKILL.md for full spec.
ExtensionAPI provides pi.registerTool(), pi.registerCommand(), pi.exec(), pi.sendMessage(), pi.sendUserMessage(). Tools use TypeBox (Type.*) for parameter schemas.

Cleave extension fully implemented at extensions/cleave/ (index.ts + assessment.ts, conflicts.ts, dispatcher.ts, planner.ts, types.ts, workspace.ts, worktree.ts). Registered in package.json pi.extensions. Skills/prompts still exist as legacy.
Cleave dispatcher spawns child pi subprocesses via `pi -p --no-session` with prompt on stdin, running in per-task git worktrees. Supports local (Ollama) and cloud backends.

Cleave extension (`extensions/cleave/`) provides recursive task decomposition: `cleave_assess` tool (pattern-matched complexity), `cleave_run` tool (git worktree-isolated parallel dispatch), and `/cleave` command. 8 modules: index.ts, assessment.ts, conflicts.ts, dispatcher.ts, planner.ts, types.ts, workspace.ts, worktree.ts. Ported from styrene-lab/cleave Python CLI.
Cleave workspace base dir changed to ~/.pi/cleave/<slug>/; generateWorkspacePath() replaces generateWorkspaceName(). See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.
Cleave test files: assessment.test.ts, conflicts.test.ts, dispatcher.test.ts, planner.test.ts, openspec.test.ts — all in extensions/cleave/, run via `npx tsx --test extensions/cleave/*.test.ts`.

Cleave state machine: /cleave command handles assess→[openspec|llm plan]→user confirm inline; cleave_run tool manages DISPATCH→HARVEST→REUNIFY→COMPLETE/FAILED. ASSESS/PLAN/CONFIRM/REPORT phases exist in CleavePhase type but are unused in runtime transitions (reserved for resume). See extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave OpenSpec integration now reads design.md and delta specs: parseDesignFileChanges(), parseDesignDecisions(), readSpecScenarios(), buildOpenSpecContext(), openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext(). OpenSpecContext type carries decisions, fileChanges, specScenarios. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.

Cleave OpenSpec integration at extensions/cleave/openspec.ts: detectOpenSpec, listChanges, findExecutableChanges, parseTasksFile, taskGroupsToChildPlans, openspecChangeToSplitPlan, openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext, buildOpenSpecContext, parseDesignFileChanges, parseDesignDecisions, readSpecScenarios, supplementScopeFromDesign. Tests at extensions/cleave/openspec.test.ts. Wired into /cleave as fast path.

OpenSpec (Fission-AI/OpenSpec) is a spec-driven development CLI: proposal→specs→design→tasks workflow with delta-spec merge on archive. Core profiles: /opsx:propose, /opsx:apply, /opsx:archive. Expanded: /opsx:ff, /opsx:continue, /opsx:verify, /opsx:sync. Cleave integrates as execution layer for tasks.md. Repo: https://github.com/Fission-AI/OpenSpec

Cleave workspace.ts initWorkspace() accepts optional OpenSpecContext param; generateTaskFile() renders Design Context section with architecture decisions, relevant file changes, and spec scenarios as acceptance criteria per child task. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

Cleave OpenSpec fast path in /cleave command now calls openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext() instead of openspecChangeToSplitPlan(); passes change.path as openspec_change_path in the followUp prompt to cleave_run.

Cleave index.ts now exports formatSpecVerification(ctx: OpenSpecContext): string — renders post-merge spec checklist from specScenarios. Appended to report when OpenSpec context present and merge succeeded. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave cleave_run tool has optional openspec_change_path param; buildOpenSpecContext() called non-fatally at dispatch start; initWorkspace() receives OpenSpecContext; post-merge formatSpecVerification() appended to report when merge succeeded. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave OpenSpec deep integration (commit a27847c): child task files now include Design Context section with Architecture Decisions (from design.md), File Changes (from design.md), and Acceptance Criteria (from delta specs Given/When/Then scenarios). Post-merge report includes spec verification checklist. cleave_run has openspec_change_path param. workspace.ts buildDesignSection() renders the enrichment. 148/148 tests passing.

Cleave now has /assess command with 3 subcommands: /assess cleave (adversarial review → auto-fix all C/W issues → commit), /assess diff [ref] (review-only diff analysis, default HEAD~1), /assess complexity <directive> (complexity check). Bare /assess <text> falls back to complexity check. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave /assess command has tab completion via getArgumentCompletions returning ASSESS_SUBS [{cleave, diff, complexity}]. RegisteredCommand.getArgumentCompletions receives argumentPrefix string, returns AutocompleteItem[]|null. AutocompleteItem: {value, label, description?} from @mariozechner/pi-tui.

ExtensionCommandContext extends ExtensionContext with: waitForIdle(), newSession(), fork(), navigateTree() — session control methods only safe in user-initiated commands. See node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts.

User has a ~/.dotfiles repo managed with GNU Stow + Nix (Determinate Nix 3.16.0). Structure: bash/, git/, kitty/, nix/, bazzite/ packages. justfile is the sole interface; deploy.sh handles stow operations. See ~/.dotfiles/README.md.

Dotfiles stow packages: each directory mirrors $HOME structure and is symlinked via GNU Stow. Opt-in packages (e.g. bazzite) only deployed if listed in ~/.dotfiles-packages. Cross-platform: macOS + Fedora Atomic/Bazzite.
Nix packages installed to a standalone profile at ~/.local/state/nix/profile (Determinate Nix standard), not home-manager. Managed via nix/flake.nix with `just nix-apply` / `just nix-upgrade`.

Secrets extension at extensions/00-secrets/index.ts: 6-layer defense system (resolveSecret cache, output redaction, tool guard, bash guard, recipe file, local model scrub, audit log). Loads before other extensions (alphabetical). See extensions/00-secrets/index.ts.

Secrets extension recipe file at ~/.pi/agent/secrets.json stores resolution recipes only (env var name, !shell command, literal:value, or op:// ref) — never literal secret values. Resolved values cached in memory only.
Secrets extension audit log at ~/.pi/agent/secrets-audit.jsonl — append-only JSONL of all guard decisions (blocked/allowed/confirmed).

Secrets extension annotation scanning: extensions declare secrets via `// @secret NAME 'description'` and config vars via `// @config NAME 'description' [default: value]` in first 30 lines of .ts/.js files. Scanned recursively from ~/.pi/agent/extensions, ~/.pi/agent/git, and project .pi/extensions.

Secrets extension resolution order: (1) in-memory cache, (2) process.env[name], (3) recipe from secrets.json. Resolved values injected into process.env so other extensions can use process.env.X without importing.

Secrets extension sensitive path patterns: hard-block on secrets.json and audit log; confirm on .env, .ssh/, .aws/, .docker/config.json, .kube/config, .npmrc, .netrc, GCP/Azure credential paths, generic credentials/token/key files.

Secrets extension bash guard: 20+ regex patterns covering security find-generic-password, op read, pass show, vault read, env dumps, cat/jq/sed on secret files, node/python/ruby process.env dumps, base64-decode-to-shell, sh -c wrapping.

Secrets extension macOS Keychain integration: stores under service 'pi-kit/<name>' with account 'pi-kit' via execFileSync (not shell) to avoid variable expansion issues with secrets containing $, backticks, etc.
Dotfiles repo is at ~/.dotfiles (not ~/dotfiles). ENOENT was for ~/dotfiles which doesn't exist.

Dotfiles nix/flake.nix: common packages include bash, zsh, tmux, kitty, eza, jq, git, gh, neovim, just, go, nodejs, pnpm, uv, pipx, awscli2, ansible, opentofu, vault, sops, kubectl, helm, kubectx, kind, argocd, talosctl, stow, gnupg, JetBrains Mono. Linux adds podman, ollama, discord. macOS darwin list is empty (GUI casks stay in Homebrew).

Dotfiles deploy.sh: stow operations (stow/restow/delete/force with backup). Opt-in packages (currently only 'bazzite') skipped unless listed in ~/.dotfiles-packages. Reads from DOTFILES_DIR, targets HOME.
Dotfiles justfile also includes: add <package>, adopt <package> <file>, list, status, check, oh-my-bash recipes. bootstrap runs: nix-apply → oh-my-bash → force → nix-fonts → nix-apps → clean.

HF Inference API working endpoint: POST router.huggingface.co/v1/chat/completions with Authorization: Bearer $HF_TOKEN and OpenAI-compatible JSON body (model, messages, max_tokens). HF_TOKEN stored via 00-secrets extension (Keychain).
Local Ollama models available: gpt-4.1-mini:latest (chat), llama3.1:8b (chat), qwen3-embedding:0.6b (embeddings) at http://localhost:11434.

Cleave /cleave command: checks for openspec/ dir first (fast path via openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext), falls back to LLM planner if no matching change or plan has <2 groups. Change path passed to cleave_run as openspec_change_path. Wired in extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave child task files now include a 'Design Context' section when OpenSpecContext is provided: architecture decisions (all children), relevant file changes from design.md, and matching spec scenarios as acceptance criteria. Implemented in workspace.ts buildDesignSection().

Cleave now enriches child scope using design.md 'File Changes' section via supplementScopeFromDesign() and enriches child task files with design decisions, file changes, and spec scenarios via buildDesignSection() in workspace.ts. Replaces heuristic inferScope() for OpenSpec paths.

Cleave post-merge spec verification: after successful merge with OpenSpec context, formatSpecVerification() renders scenario checklist in report with instructions to run /opsx:archive when passing. Non-fatal if openspecCtx is null.
Cleave index.ts imports .js extensions (e.g. './assessment.js') but files are .ts — works under pi's jiti loader but tsx/Node ESM direct execution would fail. Not a runtime bug for pi but fragile.

Cleave cleave_run tool silently ignores children whose worktree creation failed — they are marked failed but dispatch proceeds with no error surfaced to caller until final report. Failed worktrees mean those children are skipped entirely.
Cleave /cleave command delegates planning to LLM via sendUserMessage with ask_local_model instruction — if ask_local_model tool is unavailable the workflow stalls silently with no fallback.

Cleave cleave_run: ollama list --json output is parsed assuming {models:[{name:...}]} shape but actual ollama JSON uses 'models' array with 'name' field at top level — may fail to detect local models depending on ollama version.

Cleave cleave_run merges all completed children sequentially with no conflict resolution between child branches — conflicts between siblings are only detected semantically (conflicts.ts) not resolved; merge failures are reported but execution continues anyway.
Cleave has no loadState/resume capability — state is persisted but a crashed mid-dispatch run cannot be resumed.
Cleave child process stdout/stderr accumulates unboundedly in memory — long-running children could exhaust memory (dispatcher.ts:118-119).
Critical bug fix applied: cleave `needs_assessment` routing now falls through to execute path (same as `execute`) instead of routing to full decomposition. Fixed in extensions/cleave/index.ts.

FILE_EXT_RE global regex bug fully fixed: replaced with fileExtMatches()/hasFileExt() wrappers (fresh RegExp per call) and all call sites updated. Verified idempotent across repeated calls. Fixed in extensions/cleave/assessment.ts.
Cleave dispatcher semaphore TOCTOU race fixed: replaced polling loop with AsyncSemaphore class (queue-based, no polling). Fixed in extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.
Cleave failed worktree children no longer fall back to main repoPath — dispatchSingleChild now returns early if child.status === 'failed'. Fixed in extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.
Cleave dispatcher.ts had dead writeFileSync import (never used); removed. Minor cleanup in extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.
Cleave assessment.ts exports (assessDirective, matchPattern, etc.) are named exports, not default — tsx --eval must use .then(m => ...) pattern with dynamic import to access them.

Comprehensive test suite written at extensions/cleave/assessment.test.ts covering: PATTERNS structure, matchPattern (idempotency/regex-state regression), estimateSystems, detectModifiers, calculateComplexity, effectiveComplexity, detectFlags, assessDirective (integration). Uses node:test + assert/strict pattern.

Cleave truncateHead import from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent fails under tsx (ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED) — works at runtime via pi's jiti loader but cannot be tested standalone. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.

Cleave extension warnings all resolved: #5 branch cleanup (worktree.ts), #6 workspace location → ~/.pi/cleave/ (workspace.ts), #7 comment mismatch (dispatcher.ts), #11 stop-on-failure merge with per-child reporting (index.ts), #20 output truncation via truncateHead (index.ts), #21 totalDurationSec replaces totalCostUsd (types.ts, dispatcher.ts, index.ts). All 6 warnings from adversarial review are now fixed.

Cleave worktree cleanup deletes branches after prune but still iterates `cleave/*` — if current branch is a cleave branch, `git branch -D` will fail silently (caught). Low risk in normal flow but could leave orphaned branches if HEAD is detached on one.

Cleave mergeResults label-based deduplication (notAttempted filter uses m.label === c.label) — if two children share a label the report will misclassify skipped children. Labels are kebab-cased from user input, so collisions are possible.
Cleave truncateHead replaced with truncateTail for report output (index.ts) — tail is preserved, so merge failures and conflicts at bottom of report are no longer lost on truncation.

Cleave worktree placement fixed: worktrees now go to ~/.pi/cleave/wt/<childId>-<label>/ instead of ${repoPath}/../.cleave-wt-*. cleanupWorktrees updated to match both new path and legacy .cleave-wt- locations. See extensions/cleave/worktree.ts.

Cleave OpenSpec fast-path directive-to-change matching: if no change name matches the directive slug, it now falls through to LLM planner with a message listing available changes (no silent fallback to [0]).

Cleave merge stop-on-failure: on merge failures, worktree dirs are pruned but branches are preserved for manual resolution — cleanupWorktrees skipped when mergeFailures.length > 0. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.
Cleave openspec.ts intent regex fixed: now `/##\s+Intent\s*\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n##|$)/` — handles proposals with no trailing newline. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.

Cleave openspec.ts inferDependencies() fully implemented: matches 'after X', 'requires X', 'depends on X' against sibling labels and label-as-words. Called by taskGroupsToChildPlans(). See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.
Cleave taskGroupsToChildPlans filters out groups where ALL tasks are done before dispatching — returns null if <2 active groups remain. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.
Cleave FILE_EXT_RE/FILE_EXT_PATTERN removed: replaced with FILE_EXT_SOURCE string constant + fileExtMatches()/hasFileExt() wrappers in assessment.ts. Eliminates global regex state entirely.

SKILL.md lists /cleave workflow as 'assess → plan → confirm → execute → report' (5 steps) but actual state machine has 8 phases (ASSESS→PLAN→CONFIRM→DISPATCH→HARVEST→REUNIFY→REPORT→COMPLETE) — the short form in the command table omits DISPATCH/HARVEST/REUNIFY. Minor doc inaccuracy.

Cleave remaining non-critical items: no workspace cleanup/TTL for ~/.pi/cleave/ (O2), loadState() still dead code — no resume capability (O4). O3 (SKILL.md/prompts not mentioning OpenSpec) resolved in commit 7f5e69f.

SKILL.md state machine section corrected in commit ce39c6e to reflect actual split: /cleave command handles front half, CleaveState tracks dispatch onward. Prior docs incorrectly listed linear ASSESS→PLAN→CONFIRM→DISPATCH→...→COMPLETE.
Cleave test suite: 148/148 passing across 25 suites. All 5 core modules have automated tests. Run via `npx tsx --test extensions/cleave/*.test.ts`.
parseDesignDecisions returns 'Title: ' (with trailing colon+space) when decision has no rationale line — test adjusted to use startsWith() rather than exact match.

supplementScopeFromDesign label-to-path matching requires shared vocabulary (e.g. label 'auth-module' matches path 'src/auth/...'). Generic labels like 'database' don't match abbreviated dir names like 'db'.
HF Inference API deprecated api-inference.huggingface.co (410 Gone); new router endpoint is router.huggingface.co and requires auth token even for free-tier models.
D2 diagrams: 'layers' is a reserved keyword in D2 and cannot be used as a container node name — rename to 'defense' or similar.

HF router.huggingface.co /v1/chat/completions works with OpenAI-compatible format; per-model hf-inference path (router.huggingface.co/hf-inference/models/*) returns 404. Use /v1/chat/completions with model name in body.

FLUX.1 local image generation requires diffusion-cli at ~/diffusion-cli with mflux installed — not set up on this machine. Setup: `uv init ~/diffusion-cli && cd ~/diffusion-cli && uv add mflux` or set DIFFUSION_CLI_DIR env var.

pi-coding-agent exports truncateHead/truncateTail/DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES/DEFAULT_MAX_LINES/formatSize from its tools module — accessible at runtime via jiti but not resolvable via tsx due to missing package.json exports map.
Cleave AsyncSemaphore is now exported from dispatcher.ts (with activeCount/waitingCount getters). dispatcher.test.ts imports it directly — no more local reimplementation.
Cleave openspec.test.ts now covers parseDesignFileChanges, parseDesignDecisions, readSpecScenarios, buildOpenSpecContext, openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext (23 new tests added, total 50 in file).

Dotfiles justfile recipes: install/restow/delete/force (stow), nix-apply/nix-upgrade/nix-list/nix-gc/nix-fonts/nix-apps (nix), bootstrap (full setup), clean (broken symlinks). See ~/.dotfiles/justfile.

Secrets extension tests at extensions/00-secrets/index.test.ts: covers matchSensitivePath, isSecretAccessCommand, redactString, MIN_REDACT_LENGTH, SENSITIVE_PATH_PATTERNS. Run via `npx tsx --test extensions/00-secrets/index.test.ts`.

Cleave extension migration complete and committed (eeff17a). All 8 modules load cleanly, assessment engine and planner unit-tested inline. Legacy skill/prompt updated to reference extension. No further migration tasks pending.
Cleave warnings+OpenSpec commit: 46c531b on refactor/rename-diffuse-to-render. 7 files changed, 742 insertions. All 6 adversarial warnings resolved; OpenSpec fast-path wired. Test suite 121/121.

SKILL.md and prompts/cleave.md updated (commit 7f5e69f) to document OpenSpec integration, ~/.pi/cleave/ workspace layout, 9 correct pattern names, merge-failure branch preservation, and full module map. Resolves O3 from adversarial review.

title: Demo of multi-tier inference chaining across four sources
narrative: The user asked for a live demonstration of chaining multiple inference sources together. The session explored the HF router endpoint briefly, then pivoted to a structured demo: Haiku generated an outline, a local Llama 3.1 8B model via Ollama drafted prose at zero API cost, Opus rewrote it with domain expertise, and Sonnet orchestrated the full flow. The demo successfully showed cost-aware routing across cloud tiers and local inference, though local image generation (FLUX.1/mflux) failed because diffusion-cli was not installed. An open thread is setting up the diffusion-cli environment to enable fully offline image generation.

title: Tested Hugging Face API endpoints without token
narrative: The goal was to test connectivity to a Hugging Face inference endpoint. No HF token was found in the environment, keychain, or cache, so the attempt proceeded using public/unauthenticated access. The public model listing API responded successfully (HTTP 200), but all inference endpoints—including the newly required router.huggingface.co—returned 401 Unauthorized, with the legacy api-inference.huggingface.co returning 410 Gone. The session concluded that an HF_TOKEN is now mandatory for inference; next step is configuring one via secrets.
